g.data-narration: Thread Scheduling is how operating systems decide which thread gets CPU time next among many competing threads.
g.text: Deciding which thread runs next on the CPU

g.data-narration: Modern CPUs must share time among many threads. Without scheduling, system performance would collapse instantly.
The OS uses Ready Queues and Wait Queues to manage thread lifecycle. Threads wait in queues until CPU is available.
The Scheduler selects which thread runs next. The Dispatcher performs the actual context switch between threads.
g.text: Context switch, switch to user mode
Dispatch Latency: Time to stop one thread and start another

g.data-narration: Five key criteria evaluate scheduling algorithms: CPU utilization, throughput, turnaround, waiting time, and response time.
Non-preemptive scheduling cannot interrupt running threads. Preemptive allows the OS to take CPU away for fairness.
g.text: Runs until done, blocks, or yields CPU
OS can interrupt and switch to another thread

g.data-narration: First-Come-First-Served runs threads in arrival order. Simple but causes the Convoy Effect where short threads wait behind long ones.
g.text: P1 runs 24ms, P2 waits 24ms, P3 waits 27ms

g.data-narration: Shortest Job First picks shortest burst time. SRTF is preemptive version that can preempt a running thread for a shorter new arrival.
g.text: Problem: Starvation - long threads may wait forever!

g.data-narration: Priority Scheduling assigns priorities to threads. Higher priority threads run first, but low-priority threads may starve without aging.
Round Robin gives each thread a time quantum. After quantum expires, thread goes to back of queue. Great for interactive systems.
g.text: Quantum too small causes high context switch overhead

g.data-narration: Each algorithm has trade-offs. FCFS is simple but causes convoy effect. SJF minimizes waiting but causes starvation. RR balances fairness.
Modern OS use complex schedulers like Multi-Level Feedback Queues combining these algorithms. No single algorithm is optimal for all scenarios.
